rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on the same core phenomenon—consumer brand-extension evaluation and the role of fit/similarity versus parent-brand effects. The citing note explicitly says it refines prior fit-based accounts and identifies a boundary condition for earlier fit-mediation findings, while the cited paper is a major empirical generalization of that fit/quality literature across eight studies. This makes the citation plausibly foundational/comparative rather than an obvious incidental mention.
